pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 16 defines TARGET_FILE='PartialMontage.json'; export_result.sh line 12 references this filename
setup_task.sh line 15 and export_result.sh line 11 confirm this path; OpenBCI documentation states settings are saved to /Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings/

0-indexed array corresponding to Channels 1-3 in user interface (1-indexed). Task description line 4 states 'Turn OFF Channels 1, 2, and 3'. Verifier.py lines 87-88 checks channels[0:3] for disabled state.

0-indexed array corresponding to Channels 4-8 in user interface (1-indexed). Task description line 5 states 'Ensure Channels 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 remain ON'. Verifier.py lines 87-88 checks channels[3:8] for enabled state.

privileged_info_summary: The power_down_channels_hardware task operates on OpenBCI GUI v5 with an 8-channel Cyton configuration. Metadata correctly specifies the target settings filename as 'PartialMontage.json' saved to ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings/ (the standard OpenBCI Settings directory). The channel disable/enable configuration is correctly expressed in 0-indexed array notation: channels_to_disable [0,1,2] corresponds to user-facing Channels 1-3, and channels_to_enable [3,4,5,6,7] corresponds to Channels 4-8. The task uses a SYNTHETIC (live) session generating synthetic data (not real hardware or playback recordings). The task is designed to have users power down channels 1-3 and keep 4-8 enabled, then save this hardware configuration to the JSON settings file.
